topic: A person learns their college is closing down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the common room hummed with a dull, irritating monotony. Mark dragged his feet across the worn carpet, the sound loud in the otherwise deserted space. He stared blankly at the peeling poster advertising the upcoming homecoming dance, a monument to a time that felt increasingly distant. Every time a friendly voice called his name, he flinched, a tight knot forming in his chest. He just wanted to be anywhere else, anywhere but this echoing hall where the air smelled perpetually of stale coffee and impending doom. He picked at a loose thread on his hoodie, his jaw clenched so hard it ached. The realization, a cold, unwelcome trickle, had settled in his stomach like sludge. The university was shutting down.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Sarah muttered, slamming the textbook shut with unnecessary force. She didn't look up, her gaze fixed on the chipped paint of the windowsill. The silence in the library felt heavy, suffocating. She pushed her chair back, the scrape of metal against the tile floor sounding jarringly loud. Her hands were shoved deep into her pockets, knuckles white. A low, frustrated sigh escaped her lips, a sound laced with pure exasperation. She reread the official notice for the tenth time, the words blurring into meaningless ink. It was all just… over. She wanted to stomp somewhere, to break something, anything to shake off this oppressive sense of finality washing over her.

[story 3]

He meticulously stacked his already disorganized notes into a neat, suffocating tower on his desk. Each placement was deliberate, a small act of control in a world suddenly tilting off its axis. A deep furrow creased his brow as he traced the outline of the campus map with a fingertip, his movements stiff and jerky. The quiet in his dorm room was amplified, pressing in on him. He kept rubbing the bridge of his nose, a repetitive motion that seemed to physically drain the tension from his shoulders. The news had hit him like a physical blow, leaving him feeling heavy and profoundly unsettled. He just wanted the noise to stop.
